name: view_equal_no_marshalable_serialize_equal
reason: `lemma_same_views_serialize_the_same` only requires `view_equal` — it claims serialize equality even for non-marshalable values where `ghost_serialize` at the trait level is under-specified (only `recommends`)

name: view_equal_implies_marshalable_equivalence
reason: The external_body lemma ensures `self.is_marshalable() == other.is_marshalable()` from only `view_equal` — for usize this is correct since `view_equal` is `self@ === other@` and `is_marshalable` is `*self <= u64::MAX`, but the trust is unverified

name: tuple_serialize_len_sum
code:
proof fn phi_3_tuple_serialize_len_sum(a: u64, b: u64)
    requires
        a.is_marshalable(),
        b.is_marshalable(),
    ensures
        (a, b).ghost_serialize().len() == a.ghost_serialize().len() + b.ghost_serialize().len(),
{
}
reason: Tuple serialization is concatenation, so length is additive — but if `spec_u64_to_le_bytes` doesn't guarantee fixed-length output (8 bytes), the tuple's `is_marshalable` overflow check against `usize::MAX` could be vacuously satisfied or unsatisfied depending on unconstrained serialize lengths

name: usize_over_u64max_serialize_unconstrained
reason: For usize values above u64::MAX, `is_marshalable` is false and `ghost_serialize` delegates to `(*self as u64).ghost_serialize()` — the cast `*self as u64` is well-defined in spec mode but the external_body lemma claims serialize equality without the marshalability guard

name: view_equal_reflexive_tuple
code:
proof fn phi_5_view_equal_reflexive_tuple(t: (u64, u64))
    requires
        t.is_marshalable(),
    ensures
        t.view_equal(&t),
{
}
reason: Reflexivity of `view_equal` for tuples depends on reflexivity of component `view_equal` — for u64 this is `self@ === other@` which is trivially reflexive, but at the trait level there is no axiom requiring view_equal to be reflexive, so it's only guaranteed by the specific impl definitions
